summary: Jeff Atwood discusses the challenge of doing productive work on a laptop while actually using it on your lap, noting that real work typically requires a desk surface. He introduces a portable laptop stand (the PC Table Tote) that his mom found at a yard sale, which despite looking ridiculous, effectively solves the problem by allowing comfortable couch-based work with a proper mouse. The stand folds compactly and doubles its surface area for laptop and mouse placement.
key_points: Laptops are ironically difficult to use productively on actual laps, limiting their mobile utility
A portable laptop stand can bridge the gap between couch comfort and desk productivity
Sometimes the most effective tools look ridiculous but work well in practice
The ability to use a real mouse while on the couch significantly improves laptop productivity
money_quotes: For all their cool teeny-tiny productiveness, I've never been able to effectively use a laptop on... my lap.
To do real work, I have to find a desk or desk-like surface.
Initially I was quite skeptical – how can something this ridiculous looking actually work? But it does!
I can finally sit on the couch and work with my laptop at nearly full desktop speed, with a real mouse.
All I can tell you is, it looks ridiculous, and it's a bit expensive, but this PC Table Tote thing really works.
key_insight: The gap between laptop portability and desktop productivity can be bridged with the right accessories, even if they look absurd.
